steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student's goal is to introduce the scientist and her field of study to a new audience. A correct answer must: (1) name the scientist, (2) identify her profession or specialty, and (3) convey her field of study.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Located in Brazil, the Amazon Reef is one of the largest known coral reefs in the world.: Does not mention Camille Jazmin Gaynus at all. Fails to introduce the scientist. ✗
  • Choice B — Marine biologist Camille Jazmin Gaynus studies coral reefs, vital underwater ecosystems that provide homes to 25% of all marine species.: Names the scientist, identifies her as a marine biologist, and describes her field of study (coral reefs). Meets all three criteria. ✓
  • Choice C — Providing homes to 25% of all marine species, including up to 8,000 species of fish, coral reefs are vital underwater ecosystems and thus of great interest to marine biologists.: Describes coral reefs and the field but does not name or introduce Camille Jazmin Gaynus. ✗
  • Choice D — As Camille Jazmin Gaynus knows well, coral reefs are vital underwater ecosystems, providing homes to thousands of species of fish.: Names the scientist but does not identify her as a marine biologist or describe her field of study specifically — it merely implies familiarity with coral reefs. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
